definition: Equity multiples (S&P 500 ~21x forward) embed ~6-7% nominal growth incompatible with 0.3% labor force growth. Credit spreads price benign defaults despite addbacks embedding 1990-2019 conditions. Rates have partially repriced (ACM term premium from -50bp to +50-100bp). Either equities/credit are pricing AI productivity acceleration, or they are behind the rates market.
evidence: US labor force growth at 0.3% is incompatible with embedded equity growth assumptions
Credit spreads price benign defaults despite changed labor market conditions
ACM term premium shifted from -50bp to +50-100bp showing rates partial repricing
open_questions: Are equities correctly pricing AI as the demographic offset?
Which market (equities, credit, or rates) will reprice first?
Can the inconsistency persist for years or is it unstable?
